archimate:DataObject.name: Golden Record
archimate:DataObject.documentation:
Definition: "Golden Record" refers to a single, authoritative, and complete version of a data entity or a set of data that serves as the reference source for all other copies or instances of that data
Source: Eurpean Comission Data Management
Source reference: https://ec.europa.eu/research/participants/docs/h2020-funding-guide/cross-cutting-issues/open-access-data-management/open-access_en.htm
Additional information: In the context of data management, a Golden Record is the definitive copy of a data entity which may include customer profiles, product information, or any other type of organizational data. This record is created by integrating, cleansing, and consolidating data from various sources to ensure that it is the most reliable source of truth. The Golden Record is essential in scenarios where consistent data quality is critical, such as in customer relationship management (CRM), enterprise resource planning (ERP), or master data management (MDM) systems. It is used to provide a consistent and accurate view of data across different business units and applications, thereby reducing errors, improving decision-making, and enhancing operational efficiency.
Example: The following implementation is an example of how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): EU-ERS: Master Data Register. https://circabc.europa.eu/ui/group/3cc8c417-0f2a-4eb4-8ff7-10d60638446a/information
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/GoldenRecordDataObject
ABB name: egovera:GoldenRecordDataObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/GoldenRecordDataObject
Name: Golden Record
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataSetCatalogueDataObject

properties.value: "Golden Record" refers to a single, authoritative, and complete version of a data entity or a set of data that serves as the reference source for all other copies or instances of that data

The following implementation is an example of how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): EU-ERS: Master Data Register. https://circabc.europa.eu/ui/group/3cc8c417-0f2a-4eb4-8ff7-10d60638446a/information

In the context of data management, a Golden Record is the definitive copy of a data entity which may include customer profiles, product information, or any other type of organizational data. This record is created by integrating, cleansing, and consolidating data from various sources to ensure that it is the most reliable source of truth. The Golden Record is essential in scenarios where consistent data quality is critical, such as in customer relationship management (CRM), enterprise resource planning (ERP), or master data management (MDM) systems. It is used to provide a consistent and accurate view of data across different business units and applications, thereby reducing errors, improving decision-making, and enhancing operational efficiency.
